name: Mahatma Gandhi
facts: Mahatma Gandhi was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize five times between 1937 and 1948 but never received it.
He spent a total of 21 years in South Africa, where he first developed his philosophy of Satyagraha or non-violent resistance.
Gandhi traveled a total distance of approximately 79,000 kilometers across India during the struggle for independence, mostly by third-class railway compartments.
The title 'Mahatma' which means 'Great Soul' was bestowed upon him by Rabindranath Tagore, although the exact origin is sometimes debated.
Gandhi maintained a strict regimen of self-discipline, including spinning his own khadi cloth on a charkha as a symbol of economic self-reliance for India.
